message:
fix(sync): prevent broadcast-cancelled Notify waiter from leaking a stored token, add pool tests

Notify: When notify_waiters() broadcasts to all current waiters, a waiter
that is subsequently cancelled (dropped before polling to Ready) was
incorrectly converting its notification into a stored notify_one token via
the Drop forwarding logic. This caused a spurious wake for the next future
waiter — a ghost notification that should not exist because notify_waiters
is edge-triggered for the current generation only.

Fix: tag each waiter entry with the generation at which it was notified.
In Notified::drop, distinguish broadcast notifications (generation changed)
from targeted notify_one notifications (generation unchanged). Only forward
to the next waiter for targeted notifications; broadcast cancellations are
silently discarded. Adds regression test confirming that a new waiter
registered after a broadcast with a cancelled participant correctly remains
pending rather than consuming a phantom stored token.

Pool: Add test coverage for the PoolStats::total fix (verifying it includes
in-flight creating slots), Drop delegation to return_inner (no double-send
on the return channel), and compile-time Send auto-derivation assertion for
PooledResource<R: Send>.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
